highlights: Richmond swept the Kelowna Packers 4-0 in the Fred Page Cup / BCJHL final series.
Richmond swept the Quesnel Millionaires of the Peace-Cariboo Junior Hockey League two games to none to win the Mowat Cup.

Richmond opened the Western Canada Centennial Cup semifinal (Doyle Cup) with an 11-4 rout of the Red Deer Rustlers at the Richmond Arena in front of about 1,250 fans — the Sockeyes' 16th consecutive post-season win.

Dave Tomlinson opened the scoring 21 seconds in; defenceman Matt Hervey doubled the lead moments later. Red Deer's Kevan Melrose and Greg Pulliam replied on the power play to make it 4-2 after the first period. Rich Anderson narrowed it to 4-3 early in the second before Richmond pulled away.
Richmond outshot Red Deer 60-34. Frank Romeo started in goal for Richmond; Glen Furgason started for Red Deer.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 8 1987, p.21, image 558334501.
Richmond rallied from a 4-3 third-period deficit to beat Red Deer 6-4 at the Richmond Arena and take a 2-0 series lead. The win was the Sockeyes' 17th consecutive post-season victory.

Red Deer led 4-3 midway through the third when defenceman Keith Lightbown was ejected for a five-minute major after pummelling Dave Tomlinson (who never dropped his gloves). Richmond scored three goals on the ensuing power play: Dean Rutledge tied it at 9:52, Matt Hervey (his third of the game) got the winner at 11:19, and Brian Kozak added insurance 88 seconds later.

Red Deer's Dixon Ward opened the scoring midway through the first; Hervey answered on a late power play. Ken Weiss started in goal for Red Deer in place of Furgason and was strong through two periods.
Post-game, Red Deer players Rich Anderson and Brad Wilson were involved in an altercation with Richmond fans; Anderson received a gross misconduct and Wilson a match penalty.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 9 1987, p.1 and p.11, images 558334781 and 558334839.
Red Deer beat Richmond 6-3 at the Red Deer Arena to cut the series lead to 2-1. Goaltender Ken Weiss was outstanding for the Rustlers.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 13 1987, p.7, image 558335628.

Red Deer won 7-1 at the Red Deer Arena to tie the best-of-seven series 2-2. A chippy contest featured 392 penalty minutes (221 to Richmond) and a 30-minute second-period delay caused by four on-ice fights and a fracas behind the Richmond bench instigated by unruly fans throwing debris (including eggs).

Brian Puhalsky, Tracy Georgsen and Rich Anderson staked Red Deer to a 3-0 lead; Dixon Ward, Bob Duchak and Anderson added to it in the second. Dave Tomlinson got Richmond on the board at 1:39 of the third — his fourth goal of the series — but Ken Weiss shut the door the rest of the way.
The CAHA warned that continuing fan misconduct could force the series to be awarded, cancelled, or moved to a neutral site.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 13 1987, p.1 and p.7, images 558335595 and 558335628.

Red Deer beat Richmond 7-5 in overtime at the Red Deer Arena to take a 3-2 series lead. Dixon Ward scored the winner 59 seconds into the 10-minute OT; an empty-net marker followed 14 seconds after Jason Phillips was stopped on a penalty shot with 24 seconds left.
With the CAHA monitoring, the home crowd (about 1,700) was notably better-behaved. Red Deer led 3-2 after one and 4-3 after two. Ken Weiss stopped 46 Richmond shots.
Late in OT, referee Cliff Stauble awarded Phillips a penalty shot after ruling a Rustler had intentionally dislodged the net. Weiss stopped Phillips' low shot with his pad.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 15 1987, p.21, image 558335948.
Richmond beat Red Deer 7-3 before 2,020 fans at the Red Deer Arena to force a deciding seventh game, preventing the Rustlers from clinching the Doyle Cup at home.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 16 1987, p.11, image 558336101.
Richmond won the Doyle Cup with a 9-1 victory at the Red Deer Arena in the seventh and deciding game, earning the right to meet Humboldt in the Abbott Cup final.

After a scoreless first period, Richmond scored five unanswered goals in the second and never looked back. Pete deGraaf spoiled goaltender Jamie Stewart's shutout bid at 13:14 of the third, deflecting a Greg Pulliam point shot on a Red Deer power play.

Dave Tomlinson recorded a hat trick; single goals came from Rob Clarke, Mike Claringbull, Jason Phillips, Jim Gunn (per Red Deer Advocate report, with additional scorers continuing beyond the OCR break).

Rustler coach Larry Billows said his team — playing their 27th playoff game in 47 days — simply ran out of gas. Richmond coach Orland Kurtenbach said the Sockeyes were the better club in the final three games.
Source: Red Deer Advocate, Apr 18 1987, p.1 and p.12, images 558336223 and 558336261.
Richmond defeated the Humboldt Broncos to win the Abbott Cup, the Western Canadian Junior A championship.

Game 6 (Apr 28 at Humboldt): Humboldt 4, Richmond 3 in 10-minute full-time overtime. Paul Rutherford scored twice for Richmond; Dave Tomlinson scored the other Sockeyes goal. Tony Bobbitt took a penalty for closing his hand on the puck; Steve Jaques received a 5-minute match penalty for a headbutt on Bill McDougall. Bruce Hoffort made 42 saves as Humboldt was outshot 46-45. Source: Nanaimo Daily News Apr 29 1987, image 325077439.
Richmond opened the Centennial Cup tournament with a 7-3 win over the Dartmouth Fuel Kids.
Richmond defeated Pembroke 4-3 to move to 2-0 in round-robin play.
Dave Tomlinson scored two third-period goals to stop the Lumber Kings.
Source: Star-Phoenix May 5 1987, p.15, image 512094454.
Host Humboldt Broncos defeated Richmond 6-1 in the round-robin finale. Humboldt went 3-0 in round-robin; Richmond finished 2-1.
Bill McDougall opened the scoring on a power-play rebound at 6:17 of the first period. Stan Czenczek tied it on a slap shot past Bruce Hoffort at 17:26.
Garnet Kazuik broke the tie with a shorthanded breakaway at 13:20 of the second period after stealing the puck from Steve Jaques. Jamie Stewart was in net for Richmond.
Source: Star-Phoenix May 7 1987, p.17, image 512096770.
Richmond defeated Pembroke 9-3 at the Humboldt Uniplex in the Centennial Cup semifinal (8:30 PM Thursday).
Source: Star-Phoenix May 8 1987, p.29, image 512097450.
Richmond defeated Humboldt 5-2 at the Humboldt Uniplex on Saturday night (7:30 PM) to win the Centennial Cup, the national Junior A championship. Crowd approximately 2,400.

Goals — 1st period: Richmond — Tomlinson (Rutherford, Claringbull) 16:53; Richmond — Phillips (Hardy) 18:13. 2nd period: Humboldt — Chamberlin (Bergen) 10:47 PP. 3rd period: Richmond — Rutherford (Rutledge) 8:48; Richmond — Phillips (Kozak) 13:28; Humboldt — Luke (Bergen, Rice) 16:31; Richmond — Phillips (Kozak) 19:07 empty net.
Jason Phillips recorded a hat trick. Paul Rutherford scored the eventual winning goal.
Goaltender Frank Romeo made 44 saves on 46 shots; Humboldt outshot Richmond 46-23 (16-7, 11-6, 19-10). Power plays: Richmond 0/3, Humboldt 1/5. Bruce Hoffort was in goal for Humboldt.
Frank Romeo was named the game's MVP and the Centennial Cup tournament MVP — his third player-of-the-game award of the tournament across three starts.
Jason Phillips was named the tournament's Most Gentlemanly Player.

Sources: Star-Phoenix May 11 1987 p.13 (image 512098529) and p.14 box score (image 512098543); Vancouver Sun May 11 1987 p.14 (image 495230735) and p.20 (image 495229991); Times Colonist May 10 1987 p.1 (image 508633163) and p.9 (image 508632785).
